title: The Love List
excerpt: Welcome to the 2nd Two Peas Gardener's Digest on the 22nd of the 2nd month of 2012. It's a little feature that will be appearing regularly here every month where various Garden Girls will be participating, and there might even…
body:
Welcome to the 2nd Two Peas Gardener's Digest on the 22nd of the 2nd month of 2012. It's a little feature that will be appearing regularly here every month where various Garden Girls will be participating, and there might even be a little prize at the end of the blog hop.

Last time I showed you a step-by-step on how a layout came together. This time I thought I would give you a random list of things I love. Here's a little more insight into this layout, the Love List, copyright Two Peas In A Bucket.

This layout features our hand-sewn love tag, exclusive to Two Peas, as well as my favorite set of washi tapes (which seems to be sold out everywhere else), this adorable stamp set by Lawn Fawn and simple use of baker's twine. By the way, the free shipping for orders over $50 code thru the end of  February is: V14DAY.

The photos were printed at home using the sticker layout on Printsgram. They are little and adorable - about 1 inch squares. I am going to place an order for little prints (I think they are about 2 inch squares) from Printstagram this week. And I am still loving Extragram, which I mentioned in detail in this post. Hope you iPhone users out there found this to be helpful!

Also, these are some layouts that are on my love list:

Stephanie Howell rocks the doilies, and this layout where she used our Love tag is no exception:

Adore this fun & colorful layout by Laura Vegas:

Digging the tone-on-tone action on Marcy Penner's layout:

This layout by Sarah caught my eye too:

Thrilled to have this giveaway - Two Peas In A Bucket is now offering workshops! The first one is by the super talented, Shimelle and it's called The Hitchhiker's Guide to Scrapbooking.

Are you drowning in a sea of scrapbook supplies and a plethora of photos, but wish things just came together easily?  Then The Hitchhiker's Guide to Scrapbooking is just what you need: your guide to life, the universe and pretty paper! Join Garden Girl Shimelle Laine for this self-paced workshop you can use any time.  Over five chapters, she'll share forty-two brand new scrapbook pages and easy-to-follow advice on choosing supplies, designing pages, embellishing your work and writing your memories.  Each chapter includes a printable PDF and coordinating video, plus the guide's bonus reference section includes sketches, supply lists and other handy hints.  Designed for scrapping at home or at a crop, The Hitchhiker's Guide to Scrapbooking reminds you: Don't Panic - get crafting!

The content of this workshop will be available as soon as you purchase!  The lessons are available for you to work at your own pace and upon purchase can be accessed in the Workshops section under Classes & Events.  A private message board forum is also available to ask questions for Shimelle and to connect with other participants.  Enjoy!

The giveaway is a $25 value!

If you've purchased the class no worries - you have a chance to be given your money back, or you can pass the spot on to a friend!

Just leave a comment (preferably with your two peas username), and a winner will be announced on Saturday, February 25th

.Next stop on the hop is Becky Novacek.
